title: Accounting Firms and Payroll in Jamaica — Outsource or Use Software?

html:
Every Jamaican business owner faces the same question when they hire their first employee: who handles the payroll? The two most common answers are an accounting firm or payroll software. Both can work. Both have real trade-offs. This guide gives you the honest breakdown.

What Accounting Firms Actually Do for Payroll in Jamaica

Most accounting firms in Kingston and Montego Bay offer payroll as a bundled service alongside bookkeeping, audit, and tax returns. When you engage them for payroll, they typically:

  • Collect your employee hours/salary data each month (usually via email or WhatsApp)
  • Calculate PAYE, NIS, NHT, and Education Tax deductions
  • Prepare the S01 monthly remittance forms
  • Make the payments on your behalf (if you give them banking access) or send you a payment schedule
  • File the annual S01 return and assist with TD4 preparation

What they typically do not do: give you real-time payroll data, employee self-service access, digital payslips, or instant recalculation if an employee's hours change mid-month.

What Payroll Software Does

Dedicated payroll software like PayrollJamaica automates the same calculations — PAYE, NIS, NHT, Education Tax — but with key differences:

  • You enter data once; calculations happen in seconds
  • Payslips generated automatically and emailed to employees
  • S01 reports generated instantly each month
  • Rates and thresholds (like the J$1,500,096 PAYE threshold) updated automatically
  • Full payroll history accessible any time, from any device
  • Costs a fixed monthly fee, not per-hour accounting rates

Cost Comparison: Accounting Firm vs Payroll Software

ServiceTypical Cost (Jamaica, 2026)What's Included
Accounting firm payroll (5 employees)J$15,000–25,000/monthMonthly S01, basic payslips, annual return
Accounting firm payroll (20 employees)J$40,000–70,000/monthSame as above
PayrollJamaica software (5 employees)From J$3,500/monthUnlimited runs, payslips, S01 reports, compliance updates
PayrollJamaica software (20 employees)From J$8,500/monthSame as above + multi-user access

Note: Accounting firm costs vary significantly. Some Kingston firms charge less for basic payroll; others bundle it into retainer fees. Ask for a line-item breakdown before signing.

The Compliance Risk Question

Both options can be fully TAJ-compliant. But the risk profile is different:

Accounting Firm Risks

  • Human error: If your accountant uses the wrong threshold or forgets a rate update, the error flows directly to your remittance. You are legally responsible — not the firm.
  • Timing delays: If the firm is busy during tax season (February–March), your S01 might go in late. Late S01 = 50% penalty on tax due.
  • Staff turnover: If your contact leaves, the new person may not know your payroll history.
  • No audit trail: Paper or email-based workflows make it hard to reconstruct past payrolls if TAJ audits you.

Payroll Software Risks

  • You own the process: Software calculates correctly, but you must enter data accurately. Garbage in, garbage out.
  • Updates depend on the vendor: If the software provider doesn't update the PAYE threshold after a Budget announcement, you'll calculate incorrectly. Use a Jamaica-specific provider that monitors TAJ announcements.
  • Learning curve: First-time users may need 1–2 runs to get comfortable.

When an Accounting Firm Makes Sense

Accounting firm payroll is the right choice when:

  • You have complex payroll (commission structures, multi-location, expatriate employees with different tax arrangements)
  • You want one firm handling payroll, bookkeeping, and audit together — with a single point of accountability
  • You have fewer than 3 employees and the volume doesn't justify software
  • You or your team have zero time to learn any new system

When Payroll Software Makes Sense

Payroll software is the right choice when:

  • You have 3 or more employees on a regular payroll cycle
  • You want to see payroll data in real time without waiting for an accountant to reply
  • You want to cut costs — software is typically 60–80% cheaper than accounting firm fees at the same employee count
  • You want employees to receive digital payslips automatically
  • You want to manage payroll yourself, without dependency on a third party

The Hybrid Approach (Popular Among Growing Jamaican Businesses)

Many Jamaican businesses in the 10–50 employee range use a hybrid model:

  • Payroll software handles monthly calculations, payslips, and S01 remittances
  • Accounting firm reviews the annual S01 return and TD4s, and handles corporate tax

This keeps monthly costs low while retaining professional oversight for the high-stakes annual filings.

What Jamaica's Accounting Firms Are Recommending

Increasingly, Jamaican accounting firms are recommending that clients use payroll software for monthly processing, with the firm providing advisory support. This model is already standard in the UK and US — and it's gaining traction in Jamaica as more reliable, Jamaica-specific options become available.

Firms that partner with PayrollJamaica offer their clients a seamless handoff: the software handles compliance automatically, and the accountant focuses on strategy and year-end filing.

If your current accounting firm still runs payroll manually in spreadsheets or handles it all through email attachments, it may be time to ask them whether a software integration is available.

Bottom Line

For most Jamaican small businesses with 5–50 employees on a regular pay cycle, dedicated payroll software delivers better compliance, lower cost, and faster access to data than a traditional accounting firm. The savings alone — often J$200,000–500,000 per year — justify the switch for most businesses within the first three months.

For complex, multi-entity, or high-volume payrolls, a hybrid model (software + accounting firm oversight) typically delivers the best outcome.
